title: Polyalthia suthepensis
uses: Polyalthia suthepensis has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are used in traditional medicine in Thailand. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a candidate for treating various health issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses. Some studies suggest that extracts from Polyalthia suthepensis may have antioxidant and cytotoxic properties. Further investigation is necessary to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.
taxonomy: Polyalthia suthepensis is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family is part of the larger order Magnoliales. The genus Polyalthia is a genus of trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. Members of the genus Polyalthia are characterized by their compound leaves and small, often fragrant flowers. Polyalthia suthepensis is a species of tree that is endemic to Thailand.
